chunk:
CHUNK:019/021
I blinked, rubbed my eyes, and in the early morning light I saw icebergs in the fjord, and, working back to the shore, I saw the Zodiac I had borrowed still parked on the beach, and Rachael, still wearing her yellow jacket, bobbing about inside the boat. More heavily clad men spilled out of the helicopter and charged towards her. I played to my strengths and my light weight, and ran, determined to head them off. \u201cPetra, wait!\u201d Gaba again, but I didn\u2019t look. I kept going, not slowing for a second. I saw Rachael climb over the side of the boat and push it into the water. The motors were still up, and I guessed the growling I had heard must have been Gaba\u2019s boat. Rachael heaved, and the Zodiac slid deeper into the water, and she scrambled aboard as the boat bobbed, caught by the wind, and drifted into the brilliant, deep blue waters of Qinngua. \u201cConstable!\u201d I should have stopped, but something told me that if I did, something bad was going to happen to Rachael, and I picked up the pace, stripping away from the battle-ready men with their big guns, vests, and helmets, and racing onto the beach. \u201cDon\u2019t do it, honey,\u201d Rachael said as I splashed into the water. \u201cYou have to stop. They\u2019re going to shoot, Petra. Don\u2019t\u2026\u201d Rachael ducked as the men fired the first warning shots above her head. They would aim at the boat next, with the intent to puncture and sink it. Unless they had already tried, and they are lousy shots. I might have giggled at that thought, or it might have been the effect of the freezing fjord water reaching my midriff. But when I lunged for the boat, I caught a grip on the safety lines looped along the sides. And then, after another volley of warning shots, Rachael reached over the side and grabbed my wrist. I kicked my feet as she hauled me over the side, until we were both sprawled on Zodiac\u2019s deck, faces pressed to the ribbed metal surface as the men on the beach blasted the buoyancy tubes. Air hissed out of the tubes as they punctured, and Rachael laughed. \u201cWhat\u2019s so funny?\u201d I said, shouting above the noise of the guns. \u201cNothing,\u201d she said. \u201cOh, come on.\u201d I reached for Rachael\u2019s hand. \u201cDon\u2019t leave me in suspense.\u201d \u201cOkay,\u201d Rachael said, following another laugh. \u201cIt sounds like I\u2019ve got a bad case of gas.\u201d She rolled her eyes and laughed again. And I joined her, just as another bullet burst another compartment of air. \u201cSo, Constable,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cWhat do we do now?\u201d \u201cFirst,\u201d I said, pausing as the men on the beach were given the order to cease fire. \u201cFirst, you need to know you didn\u2019t hurt me. Okay?\u201d \u201cOkay.\u201d Rachael nodded. \u201cAnd second?\u201d I shook my head and said, \u201cI have no idea.\u201d \u201cWhat?\u201d \u201cNo,\u201d I said. \u201cNone.\u201d \u201cYou mean, you ran through a storm of bullets, and dived into the frickin\u2019 Arctic Ocean to get on a boat with more holes than a Swiss cheese, and you don\u2019t have a plan?\u201d \u201cNo,\u201d I said, and sighed. \u201cJesus, honey, you are nuts.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cI get that a lot.\u201d I lifted my head \u2013 just a little \u2013 to peek over the rapidly deflating tubes, and glimpsed Gaba on the beach, hands on his hips, with Saarullik and Taateraaq on each side. Gaba stared straight at me, while the two constables had their backs to the water and their weapons casually pointing at a small group of heavily armed Americans on the beach. \u201cPetra?\u201d Gaba shouted my name from the beach. \u201cYes?\u201d I lifted my head a little further and then stood up slowly. \u201cThe boat\u2019s drifting, Constable. We\u2019re coming to pick you up.\u201d \u201cGot it,\u201d I said. Gaba paused for a second before shouting, \u201cAre you okay?\u201d \u201cYes.\u201d \u201cBoth of you?\u201d \u201cWe\u2019re fine,\u201d I said. I watched as another man wearing plain clothes, some might call bland, picked his way between the men on the beach to stand next to Gaba. He carried no weapons but had an impressive bullhorn in his right hand. He held it to his lips and pressed the button to project his voice across the fjord. \u201cRachael Silk? Can you hear me?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d Rachael said as she stood up. \u201cDo you have what we agreed on?\u201d \u201cYes.\u201d Rachael removed her jacket and held it over the side of the boat, soon to be a raft. I turned around and held my breath, suddenly aware of what she was threatening to do. \u201cI\u2019ve got it. Now, are you going to give me what you promised?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d the man said. He lowered the bullhorn for a second as if he was collecting his thoughts and then pressed it to his lips again. \u201cBut you\u2019re going to have to trust me, Rachael.\u201d \u201cI can\u2019t do that, Barry.\u201d Barry? There was something about that name. \u201cBut we had a deal,\u201d Barry said. \u201cRemember?\u201d \u201cI remember lots of deals. But I don\u2019t remember ever getting what I wanted.\u201d Rachael held the jacket out a little farther over the side. \u201cMaybe it\u2019s time to renegotiate?\u201d \u201cI can\u2019t do that, Rachael,\u201d Barry said. \u201cIt\u2019s not an option. It\u2019s non-negotiable, I\u2019m afraid.\u201d \u201cWell, shit, Barry,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cThen I guess there\u2019s nothing more to say.\u201d Rachael tossed the jacket over the side of what was left of the boat, and I watched Paniula\u2019s diary fly out of the pocket. The pages from the guestbook scattered in the wind as the diary slapped onto the water\u2019s surface. \u201cNo!\u201d I moved forward, shoving Rachael to one side as I leaped over the flat buoyancy tubes and dived into the water. \u201cPetra!\u201d I don\u2019t know if it was Gaba who shouted. It might have been Rachael.
compressed_context:
MORNING LIGHT ∧ ICEBERGS IN FJORD ∧ ZODIAC-PARKED ON BEACH
PETRA RUBS EYES→ SEES ZODIAC WITH RACHAEL(YELLOW-JACKET) BOBBING
HELICOPTER-LAND → ARMED-MEN CHARGE ZODIAC
PETRA(WT=LIGHT) RUNS TO INTERCEPT; GABA: "PETRA, WAIT!" IGNORED
RACHAEL CLIMBS-OVER-BOAT-SIDE ∧ PUSHES ZODIAC INTO WATER; MOTORS↑ ∧ GROWL=GABA'S BOAT
ZODIAC DRIFTS→QINNGUA-WATERS
MEN FIRE WARNING-SHOTS ABOVE; INTENT=PUNCTURE/SINK BOAT
PETRA LUNGES→ GRIPS SAFETY-LINES
ANOTHER VOLLEY; RACHAEL GRABS PETRA WRIST; BOTH COLLAPSE ON DECK
MEN PUNCTURE BUOYANCY-TUBES; AIR HISS
RACHAEL LAUGHS; PETRA: "WHAT'S FUNNY?" RACHAEL: "BAD CASE OF GAS" ∧ BOTH LAUGH
BULLET BURSTS ANOTHER TUBE
RACHAEL: "CONSTABLE, WHAT DO WE DO NOW?"
PETRA: "FIRST, YOU DIDN'T HURT ME"; "SECOND, I HAVE NO IDEA"
RACHAEL: "YOU RUN THROUGH BULLETS ∧ DIVE ARCTIC ∧ BOARD HOLED-BOAT ∧ NO PLAN? NUTS"
PETRA: "YES, I GET THAT A LOT"
PETRA PEEKS OVER DEFLECTING-TUBES→ SEES GABA(HANDS-HIPS)
SAARULLIK+TAATERAAQ POINT WEAPONS AT AMERICANS
GABA: "PETRA?" PETRA: "YES?" GABA: "BOAT DRIFTING; PICKING YOU UP" PETRA: "GOT IT"
GABA: "YOU OKAY?" PETRA: "YES" GABA: "BOTH OF YOU?" PETRA: "FINE"
PLAIN-CLOTHES-MAN(BULLHORN): "RACHAEL SILK? HEAR ME? HAVE AGREED ITEMS?"
RACHAEL: "YES" ∧ HOLDS JACKET OVER SIDE
MAN: "YOU MUST TRUST ME, RACHAEL" RACHAEL: "I CAN'T, BARRY"
BARRY: "WE HAD A DEAL. REMEMBER?" RACHAEL: "I REMEMBER DEALS; NOT GETTING WHAT I WANTED"
RACHAEL: "MAYBE RENEGOTIATE?" BARRY: "NON-NEGOTIABLE"
RACHAEL: "SHIT, BARRY; NOTHING MORE TO SAY" ∧ TOSSES JACKET+DIARY→WATER; PAGES SCATTER
PETRA SHOVES RACHAEL→ DIVES INTO WATER AFTER DIARY
UNKNOWN: "PETRA!"
